rag_rules: Fix AGREE diagnostics by resolving type errors in binary expressions and invalid helper declarations on the same line as the error.
Ensure AGREE contracts are placed in valid component type or implementation scopes based on the context of the assumptions or guarantees.
generic_rules: When AGREE reports type mismatches, align helper declarations with the expected expression types (bool, int, real).
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers within the same annex to avoid unresolved references.
Validate that all identifiers and types used in AGREE contracts are present in the AADL model or declared in the same annex.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations; implementation annexes are for assignments and bindings.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types that are not present in the AADL model or its dependencies.
Do not add architecture elements to fix AGREE type errors; instead, correct the root cause or remove invalid clauses.
irrelevant_topics: Model fusion and insertion location decisions
